content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation to Mitigate Social Biases in Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often exhibit social biases and stereotypes in generated outputs, even when explicitly prompted to be fair. Existing prompting techniques insufficiently disrupt learned associations that drive bias.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in sensitive real-world applications, mitigating their social biases is essential for fairness and societal trust. Prompt engineering is attractive as a lightweight, post-hoc intervention, but current approaches do not robustly reduce stereotype activation in open-ended generation tasks.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation (CPA): for each input prompt, we generate counterfactual variants by swapping demographic attributes (e.g., gender, race, occupation) using a template-driven rewrite module. The original and counterfactual prompts are interleaved or concatenated as input, with explicit instructions to produce outputs consistent across demographics. The method is fully prompt-based, requires no model retraining, and leverages in-context learning to induce bias-invariant reasoning.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate CPA on established datasets measuring social bias in LLMs, such as StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs, using GPT-4 as the testbed. Baselines include standard instruction prompting and anti-bias prompt templates from recent literature. Metrics include stereotype bias scores and consistency of responses across demographic-swapped prompts. Statistical significance of bias reduction will be assessed."
}
